name: Aria Operations: vCenter Adapter Collection Failing
description: The vCenter adapter in Aria Operations stopped collecting metrics after a service account password rotation, leaving dashboards showing stale or missing data.
customerScenario: Our Aria Operations dashboards are showing stale data — the last successful metric collection was about 48 hours ago. Lots of values show N/A and some alerts that should have fired didn't. We haven't changed anything in Aria Operations itself, but the infrastructure team rotated service account passwords two days ago as part of a quarterly security review.
customerFollowUp: The password rotation was across the board — Active Directory, vCenter, NSX, and a few other systems. We've confirmed vCenter and NSX got updated. But now that I think about it, Aria Operations connects to vCenter using a service account and I'm not sure if anyone updated it there too.
fixSteps: Log in to Aria Operations as an admin
Navigate to Data Sources > Integrations (or Administration > Solutions in older deployments)
Locate the VMware vSphere adapter instance — check its Collection State
Confirm it shows 'Not Collecting', 'Collection Error', or a credential-related error message
Click on the adapter instance and select Edit
In the Credential section, select the existing credential and click Edit Credential
Update the password field to match the new service account password
Save the credential, then save the adapter instance
Click Collect Now on the adapter instance to trigger an immediate collection
Confirm the Collection State changes to 'Collecting' and dashboards begin showing fresh data
hints: Aria Operations collects metrics through adapter instances, each of which stores its own credentials. If collection stops immediately after a password rotation across your environment, the most likely cause is that one or more adapter credentials were not updated. Where in the Aria Operations UI do you manage adapter instances?

Navigate to Data Sources > Integrations in Aria Operations. Find the VMware vSphere (vCenter) adapter instance and inspect its collection state. An adapter using stale credentials will show a collection error or 'Not Collecting' — the error description often references authentication or connection failure.

Adapter credentials in Aria Operations are stored independently from the source systems. Updating the password in vCenter does not update the credential Aria Operations uses to connect to vCenter — these are separate stores that must both be updated.

Open the adapter instance for editing and find the Credential section. You can edit the existing named credential directly — this is preferable to creating a new one, as it updates the password for all adapter instances sharing that credential. Enter the new password and save.

Edit the credential: Data Sources > Integrations > [vCenter adapter] > Edit > Edit Credential > update the password > Save adapter. Click Collect Now to force an immediate collection attempt. The collection state should change to 'Collecting' within 1-2 minutes and dashboards will begin populating with fresh data.
learningObjectives: Identify a stopped adapter collection by checking the adapter instance status in Aria Operations
Understand that adapter credentials are stored independently from the monitored systems
Update a stale adapter credential after a service account password rotation
Trigger a manual collection and verify that metric ingestion resumes
author: vSphere Lab Wizard starter library
